mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 7 Series 730i / 730Li engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 730i/730Li engine typically costs £800–£9,500 supply only depending on generation. M30-era engines sit at the lower end (£800–£2,500), while B48 units run £3,500–£9,500. Fitting adds £650–£1,500. Used E38 M60 engines have been listed from around £1,200 upwards. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 7 Series 730i / 730Li engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which of the six engine generations your 730i has. The M30 (E23/E32) is a classic, robust six — one of BMW's most durable engines. The M60 V8 (E32/E38) is smooth but watch for Nikasil bore wear from high-sulfur fuels. The N52 (E65/F01) is widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable engines — gaskets and water pump are the main concerns. The B48 (G11) is efficient but the cooling system requires vigilance. Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 7 Series 730i / 730Li?

acceptedAnswer.text: M60 Nikasil bore wear on the E32/E38 V8 (compression loss, misfires), N52 oil leaks and water pump failure on the E65/F01 (gaskets, cooling system), and B48 coolant leaks on the G11 (coolant pipes, oil filter housing). The N52 is the most reliable overall.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 7 Series 730i / 730Li engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and generation. Against a well-kept £6,000 E65 730i, a £4,000 recon is 67% of the car's value — borderline. Against a tired £4,500 example, the same recon exceeds our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first. For F01 N52-engined cars (2008–2015), higher vehicle values (£8,000–£15,000) make replacement more viable.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 7 Series 730i / 730Li engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: E23 (1977–1986) is M30B30, E32 (1986–1992) is M30B30, E32 (1992–1994) is M60B30, E38 (1994–2001) is M60B30, E65/E66 (2003–2008) is N52B30A/BF, F01/F02 (2008–2015) is N52B30A/BF/AF, G11/G12 (2015–2022) is B48B20B. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the M60 Nikasil bore wear, or does the engine need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Nikasil bore wear issue is best resolved by replacing the engine with a later Alusil-liner unit (£1,500–£4,000). A compression test is essential before buying any E32 or E38 730i V8. If caught early, a rebuild may be possible, but replacement is often more cost-effective.

eraMap.title: The 730i / 730Li THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: Classic SOHC six — robust but ageing; early EGR warped heads
Refined M30 — one of BMW's most durable engines
First V8 730i — smooth, but Nikasil bore issues
V8 luxury — timing chain guides and cooling system
Magnesium-block six — gaskets and water pump
Last naturally aspirated 730i — VANOS and leaks
Turbo four — efficient but coolant and oil leaks

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

rows.bestFor: Insurance jobs, warranty claims (modern cars only)

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current B48 generation, the most common quote request today. Older generations: see the Era Map above for M30, M60, and N52-specific pricing, which runs significantly lower. Used E38 M60 engines have been listed from around £1,200 upwards.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 7 Series 730i engine typically takes 10–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). V8 engines (E32/E38) and the N52's magnesium-block construction require specialist knowledge.

problems.title: M60 Nikasil Bore Wear (E32/E38 V8 1992–2001)
problems.symptoms: Loss of compression, rough idle, misfires, increased oil consumption, poor cold starting.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; if ignored, compression loss can make the engine undriveable.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: The M60 V8's Nikasil cylinder lining was damaged by high-sulfur fuels commonly sold in the UK in the 1990s. This caused bore wear and compression loss. Most affected engines were replaced under warranty, but if you're buying an E32 or E38 730i V8 today, a compression test is essential. Replacement engines with Alusil liners are available and resolve the issue.

problems.title: N52 Oil Leaks & Cooling System Failure (E65/E66/F01/F02 2003–2015)
problems.symptoms: Burning oil smell, visible oil on engine, low oil level warnings; coolant loss, overheating.
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–120,000 miles — age-related as gaskets and plastics degrade.
problems.repairCost: £300–£700 (valve cover gasket) / £200–£500 (oil filter housing gasket) / £400–£800 (water pump).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; if ignored, oil leaks can lead to oil starvation or belt failure.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: The N52 is a reliable engine, but oil leaks from the valve cover, oil filter housing, and oil pan gaskets are common. The electric water pump is also prone to failure. Oil dripping from the oil filter housing gasket can contaminate the serpentine belt, leading to belt failure and potential engine damage. If you notice a burning oil smell or coolant warnings, have the system inspected promptly.

problems.title: B48 Coolant Leaks (G11/G12 2015–2022)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, white steam from engine bay, low coolant warning, overheating.
problems.typicalMileage: 30,000–80,000 miles — coolant system components can fail early.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; if ignored, overheating can cause head gasket failure.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if the coolant warning comes on, pull over immediately.

problems.recommendation: The B48's cooling system is its most notorious weak point. Owners report major coolant leaks as early as the second year of ownership. The oil filter housing can also crack, creating coolant or oil leaks around the 50,000 to 100,000-mile mark. If you see any coolant warnings, have the system inspected immediately — these engines can overheat quickly.

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 730i / 730Li? Check These First
buyIf: * E32/E38 M60 with Nikasil issue resolved (Alusil replacement) or proven compression test
* E65/E66/F01/F02 N52 with gaskets replaced and water pump done — the most reliable modern 730i
* G11/G12 B48 with coolant system inspected and no leaks — modern and efficient
* Full service history on any generation — these flagships are unforgiving of neglect
avoidIf: * E32/E38 M60 without compression test — Nikasil bore wear can be a £4,000 surprise
* N52 with oil filter housing gasket leak — can cause belt failure and engine damage
* B48 with any coolant leaks — the cooling system is the B48's Achilles' heel
* Poor service history on any generation — these engines need regular maintenance

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 730i/730Li badge spans a remarkable range of engines — from the classic M30 six to the V8-powered E38, the refined N52, and the modern B48. The M30 (E23/E32) is a classic, robust six — one of BMW's most durable engines. The M60 V8 (E32/E38) is smooth but watch for Nikasil bore wear. The N52 (E65/E66/F01/F02) is widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable engines — gaskets and water pump are the main concerns. The B48 (G11/G12) is efficient but the cooling system requires vigilance. With the 70% BMW scrap threshold, a £4,000 recon on a £6,000 E65 730i is 67% — borderline. On a sub-£5,000 example, think carefully.

cta.label: → Compare 7 Series 730i / 730Li engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 7 Series 730i / 730Li (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or ticking on startup (timing chain or VANOS)
* Service History — Including which engine era and confirmed oil-interval discipline
* Compression Test — Essential on M60 V8 (E32/E38) — Nikasil bore wear is a known issue
* Oil Leaks — Check around valve cover and oil filter housing (N52 common)
* Coolant Check — Check level and condition — B48 coolant leaks are common
* Water Pump — Ask if the electric water pump has been replaced on N52 (especially over 80,000 miles)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
